body_txt: Bob must wear some form of uniform which he dislikes, so he tries to act out and be an individual by modifying it or wearing distinctive accessories. If he gets away with it, he may even inspire others to do the same. Bob's change to his uniform usually comes from finding a loophole — the rulebook says "black shirt, shoes, socks, and pants," but says nothing about silly hats or tearing the sleeves off the shirts . If enough people have the same idea then it may become difficult to identify what is supposed to be the default. Essentially a Dress Code, but not a strict Uniform. This is often done to make characters more visually interesting while still keeping a team feel. In real life, this is a key reason why novelty socks and neckties, unusual earrings and watches, etc., are so popular. It's especially popular with pop girl groups, either having the ladies wear the same thing in different colors, or the same color/pattern in different cuts. Say if the theme is a red top and black skirt, Alice might wear a tank top, Brittany a tube top, Cassie a halter, and Danielle a baby tee. Compare Custom Uniform , which is personal modifications for the straight Uniqueness Value , meant to highlight the main characters while ignoring background extras of Nominal Importance . This trope often applies to Superhero Team Uniforms and Mildly Military groups. Custom Uniform of Sexy is also usually this, but with a specific Fanservice angle. Also compare Transfer Student Uniforms ; "technically I'm just wearing my old uniform while I wait for the new one to be delivered, but it's also visual shorthand for my New Transfer Student status."
